verification_performed: Traced recommendation creation path in recommendation_api.py for any LATE_INNING_BLOCK read or inning-based late-entry suppression
Traced bridge approval path in model_bridge.py for any LATE_INNING_BLOCK read or inning-based rejection
Traced entry gating path in risk.py and bot_core.py to see whether inning fields are passed into active entry gates and whether any late-inning block is enforced
Checked runtime artifact context to confirm late-game trade failures existed in live history, without using it as sole proof of wiring state
observed: LATE_INNING_BLOCK is present in config: `.env` contains `LATE_INNING_BLOCK=7`.

In the audited recommendation path, recommendation_api.py fetches live game state with `snap = get_game_snapshot(...)` and includes `inning`, `inning_half`, `outs`, `score_diff`, `game_progress`, and `game_status` in the returned recommendation object.
No LATE_INNING_BLOCK config read appears in recommendation_api.py within the audited path. No inning-threshold rejection is applied before or after recommendation creation there.

No LATE_INNING_BLOCK config read appears in model_bridge.py. The bridge enforces model_version, rec_age, market type, slug date, edge, confidence, game_state_age, book_age, open_position, duplicate_batch, and entry_price parseability, but no late-inning rule.
risk.py defines the active `check_entry_gates(...)` function and contains no LATE_INNING_BLOCK read and no inning-based rejection logic.

bot_core.py calls `check_entry_gates(...)` with orderbook, signal, mode_ctx, open counts, market id, time_to_end_seconds, market, and market_cooldown. In the audited call path, it does not pass inning or inning_half into the gate function.

Because inning data exists on the recommendation object but is not consumed by model_bridge.py or risk.py for late-inning rejection, the current live recommendation-to-entry chain has no visible active enforcement point for LATE_INNING_BLOCK within the audited files.

proven.point: LATE_INNING_BLOCK is read from config only at the .env layer in the audited path, but not consumed by the active recommendation, bridge, or entry gate code that was traced.
proven.evidence: `.env` contains `LATE_INNING_BLOCK=7`, while recommendation_api.py, model_bridge.py, and risk.py show no LATE_INNING_BLOCK usage in the audited path.

proven.point: The audited active live entry path is recommendation_api.py -> model_bridge.py -> bot_core.py -> risk.check_entry_gates(...).
proven.evidence: recommendation_api.py constructs recs, model_bridge.py filters and packages intents, bot_core.py calls `check_entry_gates(...)`, and risk.py defines that gate function.

proven.point: LATE_INNING_BLOCK does not affect recommendation creation in the audited path.
proven.evidence: recommendation_api.py builds recommendations from snap, inference, and market state, but no inning-threshold branch references LATE_INNING_BLOCK.

proven.point: LATE_INNING_BLOCK does not affect bridge approval in the audited path.
proven.evidence: model_bridge.py contains freshness, edge, confidence, and duplication checks only. No inning-based block appears in the approval waterfall.

proven.point: LATE_INNING_BLOCK does not affect entry gating in the audited path.
proven.evidence: risk.py `check_entry_gates(...)` has no inning parameter use and no LATE_INNING_BLOCK logic; bot_core.py does not pass inning-derived state into that gate call.

proven.point: The break in the chain is between config and enforcement: the config value exists, inning data exists on recommendations, but no active enforcement node consumes the config to reject late-inning entries.
proven.evidence: Inning fields are attached to the recommendation object, but the later gate surfaces do not read LATE_INNING_BLOCK or apply an inning threshold.

likely: The original design intent was probably to enforce LATE_INNING_BLOCK somewhere in the game-context or entry-gate layer, but in the currently audited live path it is effectively inert.
Because inning data is present on recommendations, the smallest missing link is not data acquisition. It is wiring: no audited live gate consumes the inning field together with the config threshold.
This missing wiring likely contributed to late-game exposure, but this audit does not claim it is the only cause of near-resolution irrational entries.
ruled_out: Ruled out: LATE_INNING_BLOCK actively blocking late-inning entries in the audited recommendation path.
Ruled out: LATE_INNING_BLOCK actively blocking late-inning entries in the audited bridge approval path.
Ruled out: LATE_INNING_BLOCK actively blocking late-inning entries in the audited entry-gate path.
Ruled out: missing inning data being the reason the block is inactive, because inning data is already present on the recommendation object.
recommended_next_step: Smallest next fix target, if manager chooses to open one, is the active entry-gate boundary, not broader model work: wire a single inning-threshold check into the live recommendation-to-entry path where bot_core.py calls risk.check_entry_gates(...), or equivalently into the narrowest active gate surface that already receives recommendation context. Before any proof messaging is added, CONFIG_HASH_INPUTS_FIX_001 should still come first so runtime truth reflects real gate variables, and STARTUP_PROOF_BLOCK_001 should follow only after the hash inputs are corrected and reviewed.
queued_next_list: STARTUP_PROOF_BLOCK_001 only after config-hash fix is reviewed, if that is what the evidence supports
